mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 7.0 percent signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recession, triggering immediate risk-off selling that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer and investment demand, driving sustained equity appreciation over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative forward returns in more than 60% of cases or the interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by more than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days exclusively during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during recovery regimes or Rank IC drops below 0.03 when the regime filter is applied.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 210 reflect entrenched inflationary pressures that force aggressive monetary tightening expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of triggered instances or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (K-S test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market conditions that support consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or payroll growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail auto sales and housing starts demonstrate synchronized consumer durables and real estate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect vanishes when either series declines or the combined signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below the 100 baseline index signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakening economic momentum, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) during contraction phases.
